message:
feat(aa10): add cross-track composition gauntlet contract (AA-10.4)

Compatibility matrix (11 combos), incident drills (5), structured log
fields, smoke runner, and 39 invariant tests. Closes asupersync-1508v.10.7.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -455,7 +455,7 @@
 {"id":"asupersync-1508v.10.4","title":"[AA-10.1] Define claim/evidence/policy/trace graph schema and artifact bundle contract","description":"Define the graph schema and bundle contract that connect runtime claims to evidence, policies, traces, workloads, tests, and rollback triggers across the entire ascension program.","design":"Scope:\n- Standardize IDs and linkage rules for claims, evidence, policies, traces, workloads, and decisions.\n- Keep the schema simple enough for humans to inspect and tooling to validate.\n- Make it the default closure language for the full program.","acceptance_criteria":"1. The schema covers claim, evidence, policy, trace, workload, test, and rollback linkage.
2. Bundles can be validated deterministically, are versioned, and have direct replay or inspection commands.
3. Deterministic unit tests and bundle round-trip E2E scripts protect schema evolution and bundle validation.
4. Other ascension tracks can plug their artifacts into the schema without ad hoc mapping, and structured validation logs explain any rejection at human- and machine-readable levels.","notes":"Primary risk: schema overgrowth.\nCountermeasure: version early and keep the core graph minimal.\nFallback: allow reduced bundles but mark the linked claims non-default or provisional.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"ubuntu","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:54:04.916788136Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-06T20:21:47.342446170Z","closed_at":"2026-03-06T20:21:47.342422797Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["aa10-verification","alien-artifact","formal","runtime-ascension","verification"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.10.4","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.1.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-06T06:32:13.659683383Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.10.4","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.10","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:54:04.916788136Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.10.4","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.2.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:55:48.055629045Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.10.4","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.6.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:55:50.534639470Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"asupersync-1508v.10.5","title":"[AA-10.2] Implement CI gates for proof artifacts, calibration drift, and tail regressions","description":"Implement the hard gates that make the ascension program operationally real: proof/artifact consistency checks, calibration-drift alarms, tail-regression budgets, and rollout readiness validation.","design":"Scope:\n- Fail closed on missing or mismatched evidence.\n- Make calibration drift and tail regressions first-class CI failures.\n- Integrate with deterministic unit, E2E, and bench flows plus evidence bundles.","acceptance_criteria":"1. CI can validate artifact bundles, controller calibration state, track validation-pack coverage, composition eligibility, and tail-budget deltas.
2. Failures are actionable and point to the exact broken claim, artifact, workload, missing validation pack, or unsupported combination.
3. Deterministic unit, E2E, and bench commands plus structured logs are part of the gate, and failures emit exact rerun commands.
4. Progressive-delivery readiness is computed from explicit evidence, per-track coverage status, and composition status, not human memory.","notes":"Primary risk: noisy gates that people learn to ignore.\nCountermeasure: tie each gate to a concrete claim or invariant and tune against the AA-01 corpus.\nFallback: degraded or experimental tracks stay out of default rollout if evidence is insufficient.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"ubuntu","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:54:10.193652802Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-06T21:55:33.032285320Z","closed_at":"2026-03-06T21:55:33.032259322Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["aa10-verification","alien-artifact","ci","runtime-ascension","verification"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.10.5","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.10","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:54:10.193652802Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.10.5","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.10.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:55:53.033214189Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.10.5","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.2.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-06T06:32:16.167470743Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.10.5","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.3.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:55:55.533223182Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.10.5","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.4.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:55:58.056030570Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.10.5","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.7.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:56:00.574227580Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.10.5","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.9.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:56:03.076018953Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"asupersync-1508v.10.6","title":"[AA-10.3] Produce comparative demos, closure packet, and launch doctrine for runtime ascension","description":"Produce the final closure packet for the ascension program: comparative demos, claim-backed benchmark summaries, known-risk register, rollback doctrine, and the operator/developer guidance needed to continue or ship the work responsibly.","design":"Scope:\n- Summarize what actually improved, what remained experimental, and why.\n- Include side-by-side comparisons against conservative baselines and any valid external references.\n- Leave behind a package future contributors can extend without reconstructing the original intent.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Closure packet links every important claim to evidence, unit tests, workloads, structured logs, rollback triggers, and cross-track compatibility verdicts.
2. Comparative demos and deterministic E2E drill scripts distinguish default-ready work from experimental work and explain the user-visible difference.
3. Known risks, non-goals, downgrade paths, unsupported combinations, and next-step decisions are explicit.
4. Final docs, runnable verification commands, composition-drill recipes, structured incident logs, and operator/developer troubleshooting recipes are self-contained enough to act as the continuation and incident-response point for future sessions.","notes":"Primary risk: turning closure into marketing instead of truth.\nCountermeasure: every headline result must cite the underlying evidence graph.\nFallback: if a track lacks closure-quality evidence, it remains explicitly experimental in the launch doctrine.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:54:15.209441938Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-06T07:18:25.452896555Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["aa10-verification","alien-artifact","release","runtime-ascension","verification"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.10.6","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.10","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:54:15.209441938Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.10.6","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.10.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:56:05.571178582Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.10.6","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.10.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-06T06:55:37.874942303Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.10.6","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.5.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:56:08.062936829Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.10.6","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.6.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:56:10.619028203Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.10.6","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.8.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:56:13.114106178Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"asupersync-1508v.10.7","title":"[AA-10.4] Run cross-track composition gauntlet, compatibility matrix, and incident drill pack","description":"Execute the full-system validation layer that the per-track beads do not cover on their own: deterministic composition tests across interacting advanced features, a compatibility matrix, and operator-grade incident drill artifacts.","design":"Scope:
- Compose the graduating tracks in the combinations that matter to real users: decision plane, control artifacts, fast-path substrate, static safety surfaces, trace intelligence, authority sandboxing, crash-only recovery, and transport experiments.
- Treat compatibility as a first-class artifact rather than an assumption hidden inside other validation beads.
- Build deterministic drills for incident handling, downgrade, rollback, and replay across track boundaries.
- Produce a matrix that distinguishes supported default combinations, supported-but-experimental combinations, and forbidden combinations.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Deterministic unit and E2E composition suites cover high-risk combinations across controllers, fast paths, static surfaces, trace intelligence, authority, recovery, and transport.
2. A compatibility matrix names supported, degraded, experimental, and forbidden combinations together with explicit fallback or downgrade behavior.
3. Structured logs, incident packets, and exact rerun commands let an operator reproduce any failing composition scenario directly.
4. Any cross-track regression in invariants, tail budgets, recovery behavior, authority containment, or diagnosability blocks closure.","notes":"Why this bead exists:
- Per-track validation is necessary but not sufficient. Users experience the composed system, not isolated tracks.
- The biggest late-stage risk in an ambitious runtime program is interaction failure: features that are individually correct but jointly unstable, opaque, or operationally painful.
- This bead is the plan-space answer to that failure mode.

Fallback doctrine:
- If a combination is not proven, it is explicitly marked unsupported or experimental.
- Default-ready closure requires both per-track green status and composition-level green status.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-06T06:55:10.178075830Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-06T07:09:55.194329811Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["aa10-verification","alien-artifact","ci","release","runtime-ascension","testing","verification"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.10.7","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.10","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-06T06:55:10.178075830Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.10.7","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.10.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-06T06:55:15.179026009Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.10.7","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.2.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-06T06:55:17.692032263Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.10.7","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.3.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-06T06:55:20.216484275Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.10.7","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.4.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-06T06:55:22.736266190Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.10.7","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.5.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-06T06:55:25.237995174Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.10.7","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.6.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-06T06:55:27.734485164Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.10.7","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.6.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-06T07:09:55.194257877Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.10.7","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.7.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03- _on_id":"asupersync-1508v.7.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-06T06:55:30.257797392Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.10.7","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.8.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-06T06:55:32.763947553Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.10.7","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.9.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-06T06:55:35.339999483Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"asupersync-1508v.2","title":"[AA-02] Proof-Carrying Decision Plane Kernel","description":"Unify adaptive runtime logic behind one decision-plane kernel with explicit controller registration, artifact verification, evidence emission, rollout rules, and deterministic fallback semantics.","design":"Background:\n- The repository already contains decision-contract and adaptive-control ingredients, but they are still more like powerful islands than a single kernel.\n- This track turns controllers into first-class runtime artifacts rather than ad hoc code paths.\n\nTrack outputs:\n- Runtime snapshot/registration contract for every controller.\n- Proof-carrying artifact format and verifier.\n- Policy registry with shadow/canary/ramp/rollback behavior.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Controllers register against one explicit runtime snapshot contract with deterministic field semantics.
2. Controller artifacts carry assumptions, bounds, versioning, compatibility, signatures/hashes, and conservative fallback references.
3. Runtime loading rejects invalid or incompatible controller artifacts before they influence behavior.
4. Shadow mode, canary promotion, rollback, and evidence-ledger emission are standardized rather than reimplemented per subsystem.
5. The track emits deterministic unit and E2E validation paths, structured diagnostics, and exact rollback/rerun commands that make controller behavior inspectable for operators and future contributors.","notes":"Recommendation card:\n- Change: separate the deterministic data plane from an explicit, auditable decision plane.\n- EV: highest strategic leverage because it makes future radical controls safe to ship.\n- Primary risk: over-centralized complexity.\n- Countermeasure: narrow controller seam, typed snapshot contract, and fail-closed artifact verification.\n- Conservative fallback: current static config/runtime heuristics remain loadable and selectable as the default artifact.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:44:19.813234283Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-06T19:09:47.435739722Z","closed_at":"2026-03-06T19:09:47.435711910Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["aa02-decision-plane","alien-artifact","decision-plane","governance","runtime-ascension"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.2","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:44:19.813234283Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.2","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:45:05.519270402Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"asupersync-1508v.2.1","title":"[SUPERSEDED] [AA-02.1] Define RuntimeKernelSnapshot and controller registration contract","description":"Define the canonical runtime snapshot that controllers are allowed to observe and the registration contract every controller must satisfy before it can participate in decisions.","design":"Scope:\n- Specify snapshot fields, versioning, units, and update cadence.\n- Include mandatory controller metadata: decisions made, fallback active, calibration score, last action, artifact IDs, and budget counters.\n- Keep the seam narrow enough that the data plane remains deterministic and auditable.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Snapshot fields are versioned, typed, and documented with ownership and update rules.\n2. Registration validation rejects controllers with missing metadata or unsupported field expectations.\n3. Unit tests cover snapshot serialization, compatibility checks, and deterministic update semantics.\n4. Structured logs expose snapshot/version IDs without leaking ambient authority or nondeterministic state.","notes":"Superseded by asupersync-1508v.2.4 after an interrupted shell pass created a duplicate task. Keep this issue only as audit history; the active canonical bead is asupersync-1508v.2.4.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:49:15.427183388Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-06T05:58:08.459081115Z","closed_at":"2026-03-06T05:58:08.458964047Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["aa02-decision-plane","alien-artifact","decision-plane","formal","runtime-ascension"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.2.1","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.1.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:51:35.122774226Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.2.1","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:49:15.427183388Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"asupersync-1508v.2.2","title":"[SUPERSEDED] [AA-02.2] Implement proof-carrying controller artifact format and verifier","description":"Implement the artifact format that packages controller policy tables, assumptions, bounds, compatibility ranges, fallback references, and verification material into one deterministic load unit.","design":"Scope:\n- Define manifest schema, hash/signature chain, assumptions ledger, budget caps, and fallback pointer.\n- Implement a verifier that rejects mismatched, stale, or incompatible artifacts before runtime use.\n- Ensure artifact verification itself is deterministic and replayable.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Controller artifacts include manifest, compatibility, bounds, assumptions, and fallback metadata.\n2. Loader/verifier rejects malformed or mismatched artifacts with deterministic diagnostics.\n3. Unit and E2E tests cover happy path, signature/hash mismatch, version mismatch, and fallback activation.\n4. Structured logs emit artifact IDs, verifier verdict, and rollback pointer.","notes":"Superseded by asupersync-1508v.2.5 after an interrupted shell pass created a duplicate task. Keep this issue only as audit history; the active canonical bead is asupersync-1508v.2.5.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:49:20.405456193Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-06T05:58:13.220187243Z","closed_at":"2026-03-06T05:58:13.220073171Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["aa02-decision-plane","alien-artifact","decision-plane","runtime-ascension","security"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.2.2","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:49:20.405456193Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.2.2","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:51:37.626527356Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

@@ -0,0 +1,160 @@
+{
+  "contract_version": "cross-track-composition-v1",
+  "bead_id": "asupersync-1508v.10.7",
+  "description": "AA-10.4 cross-track composition gauntlet, compatibility matrix, and incident drill pack.",
+  "runner_script": "scripts/run_cross_track_composition_smoke.sh",
+  "runner_bundle_schema_version": "cross-track-composition-smoke-bundle-v1",
+  "runner_report_schema_version": "cross-track-composition-smoke-run-report-v1",
+  "compatibility_matrix": {
+    "description": "Explicit classification of track combinations: supported, experimental, or forbidden.",
+    "tracks": [
+      {"track_id": "AA-01", "name": "Scheduling Substrate"},
+      {"track_id": "AA-02", "name": "Decision Plane"},
+      {"track_id": "AA-03", "name": "Controller Interference"},
+      {"track_id": "AA-04", "name": "Bounded Latency"},
+      {"track_id": "AA-05", "name": "Session-Typed Safety"},
+      {"track_id": "AA-06", "name": "Trace Intelligence"},
+      {"track_id": "AA-07", "name": "Authority Sandboxing"},
+      {"track_id": "AA-08", "name": "Transport Experiments"},
+      {"track_id": "AA-09", "name": "Crash-Only Recovery"}
+    ],
+    "combinations": [
+      {
+        "combo_id": "CX-DECIDE-SCHED",
+        "tracks": ["AA-01", "AA-02"],
+        "status": "supported",
+        "description": "Controllers observe and influence scheduling via snapshots and decisions."
+      },
+      {
+        "combo_id": "CX-DECIDE-INTERFERE",
+        "tracks": ["AA-02", "AA-03"],
+        "status": "supported",

@@ -0,0 +1,51 @@
+# Cross-Track Composition Contract
+
+Bead: `asupersync-1508v.10.7`
+
+## Purpose
+
+This contract defines the cross-track composition gauntlet: a compatibility matrix classifying track combinations as supported/experimental/forbidden, incident drills exercising multi-track failure paths, and actionable structured logs for reproduction.
+
+## Contract Artifacts
+
+1. Canonical artifact: `artifacts/cross_track_composition_v1.json`
+2. Smoke runner: `scripts/run_cross_track_composition_smoke.sh`
+3. Invariant suite: `tests/cross_track_composition_contract.rs`
+
+## Compatibility Matrix
+
+| Combination | Tracks | Status |
+|-------------|--------|--------|
+| CX-DECIDE-SCHED | AA-01, AA-02 | supported |
+| CX-DECIDE-INTERFERE | AA-02, AA-03 | supported |
+| CX-DECIDE-LATENCY | AA-02, AA-04 | supported |
+| CX-DECIDE-SAFETY | AA-02, AA-05 | supported |
+| CX-DECIDE-AUTHORITY | AA-02, AA-07 | supported |
+| CX-DECIDE-RECOVERY | AA-02, AA-09 | supported |
+| CX-AUTHORITY-RECOVERY | AA-07, AA-09 | supported |
+| CX-TRACE-RECOVERY | AA-06, AA-09 | supported |
+| CX-TRANSPORT-LATENCY | AA-08, AA-04 | experimental |
+| CX-TRANSPORT-RECOVERY | AA-08, AA-09 | experimental |
+| CX-FULL-STACK | All core | supported |
+
+## Incident Drills
+
+| Drill | Tracks | Scenario |
+|-------|--------|----------|
+| ID-CONTROLLER-CRASH-RECOVERY | AA-02/07/09 | Controller causes crash, full recovery cycle |
+| ID-TAIL-REGRESSION-ROLLBACK | AA-02/04 | Tail budget exceeded, automatic rollback |
+| ID-OBLIGATION-LEAK-DETECTION | AA-05/09 | Crash before settlement, leak detection |
+| ID-AUTHORITY-REVOCATION-CASCADE | AA-07 | Root revocation cascades to all descendants |
+| ID-FULL-STACK-DOWNGRADE | AA-01/02/04/07/09 | Multi-failure graceful degradation |
+
+## Validation
+
+```bash
+rch exec -- env CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0 CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch-codex-aa104 cargo test --test cross_track_composition_contract -- --nocapture
+```
+
+## Cross-References
+
+- `artifacts/cross_track_composition_v1.json`
+- `artifacts/ci_proof_gates_v1.json` -- CI gate definitions
+- `artifacts/claim_evidence_graph_v1.json` -- Evidence graph

@@ -0,0 +1,132 @@
+#!/usr/bin/env bash
+set -euo pipefail
+
+SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
+PROJECT_ROOT="$(dirname "$SCRIPT_DIR")"
+CONTRACT_ARTIFACT="${PROJECT_ROOT}/artifacts/cross_track_composition_v1.json"
+OUTPUT_ROOT="${CTC_SMOKE_OUTPUT_DIR:-${PROJECT_ROOT}/target/cross-track-composition-smoke}"
+TIMESTAMP="$(date +%Y%m%d_%H%M%S)"
+RUN_DIR="${OUTPUT_ROOT}/run_${TIMESTAMP}"
+LIST_ONLY=0
+DRY_RUN=1
+
+declare -a SELECTED_SCENARIOS=()
+
+usage() {
+    cat <<'USAGE'
+Usage: ./scripts/run_cross_track_composition_smoke.sh [options]
+
+Options:
+  --list                    List scenario IDs and exit
+  --scenario <id>           Run one scenario (repeatable)
+  --output-root <dir>       Override output root
+  --dry-run                 Emit manifests without executing (default)
+  --execute                 Execute cargo test scenarios
+  -h, --help                Show help
+USAGE
+}
+
+require_tools() {
+    if ! command -v jq >/dev/null 2>&1; then
+        echo "FATAL: jq is required" >&2; exit 1
+    fi
+    if [ ! -f "$CONTRACT_ARTIFACT" ]; then
+        echo "FATAL: contract artifact missing at ${CONTRACT_ARTIFACT}" >&2; exit 1
+    fi
+}
+
+json_escape() { printf '%s' "$1" | sed 's/\\/\\\\/g; s/"/\\"/g'; }
+contract_version() { jq -r '.contract_version' "$CONTRACT_ARTIFACT"; }
+bundle_schema_version() { jq -r '.runner_bundle_schema_version' "$CONTRACT_ARTIFACT"; }
+report_schema_version() { jq -r '.runner_report_schema_version' "$CONTRACT_ARTIFACT"; }
+
+list_scenarios() {
+    jq -r '.smoke_scenarios[] | [.scenario_id, .description] | @tsv' "$CONTRACT_ARTIFACT" \
+        | while IFS=$'\t' read -r sid desc; do printf '%-38s %s\n' "$sid" "$desc"; done
+}
+
+load_scenario_json() {
+    jq -c --arg sid "$1" '.smoke_scenarios[] | select(.scenario_id == $sid)' "$CONTRACT_ARTIFACT"
+}
+
+RESULTS_JSON=""
+append_result() {
+    if [[ -z "$RESULTS_JSON" ]]; then RESULTS_JSON="$1"; else RESULTS_JSON="${RESULTS_JSON},$1"; fi
+}
+
+run_scenario() {
+    local sid="$1" scenario_json description command scenario_dir log_file summary_file started_ts ended_ts status rc
+    scenario_json="$(load_scenario_json "$sid")"
+    [[ -z "$scenario_json" ]] && { echo "FATAL: unknown scenario: $sid" >&2; return 1; }
+    description="$(jq -r '.description' <<<"$scenario_json")"
+    command="$(jq -r '.command' <<<"$scenario_json")"
+    scenario_dir="${RUN_DIR}/${sid}"; log_file="${scenario_dir}/run.log"; summary_file="${scenario_dir}/bundle_manifest.json"
+    mkdir -p "$scenario_dir"
+    started_ts="$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)"
+    echo ">>> Running scenario ${sid}"
+    if [[ "$DRY_RUN" -eq 1 ]]; then
+        printf 'DRY_RUN scenario=%s\n' "$sid" >"$log_file"; rc=0; status="dry_run"
+    else
+        rc=0; eval "$command" >"$log_file" 2>&1 || rc=$?
+        status="$( [[ "$rc" -eq 0 ]] && printf "passed" || printf "failed" )"
+    fi
+    ended_ts="$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)"
+    cat >"$summary_file" <<JSON
+{
+  "schema_version": "$(json_escape "$(bundle_schema_version)")",
+  "contract_version": "$(json_escape "$(contract_version)")",
+  "scenario_id": "$(json_escape "$sid")",
+  "description": "$(json_escape "$description")",
+  "status": "$(json_escape "$status")",
+  "exit_code": ${rc},
+  "started_ts": "$(json_escape "$started_ts")",
+  "ended_ts": "$(json_escape "$ended_ts")"
+}
+JSON
+    append_result "$(jq -c '.' "$summary_file")"
+    [[ "$rc" -eq 0 ]]
+}
+
+while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do
+    case "$1" in
+        --list) LIST_ONLY=1; shift ;;
+        --scenario) SELECTED_SCENARIOS+=("${2:-}"); shift 2 ;;
+        --output-root) OUTPUT_ROOT="${2:-}"; RUN_DIR="${OUTPUT_ROOT}/run_${TIMESTAMP}"; shift 2 ;;
+        --dry-run) DRY_RUN=1; shift ;;
+        --execute) DRY_RUN=0; shift ;;
+        -h|--help) usage; exit 0 ;;
+        *) echo "Unknown: $1" >&2; usage >&2; exit 1 ;;
+    esac
+done
+
+require_tools
+if [[ "$LIST_ONLY" -eq 1 ]]; then list_scenarios; exit 0; fi
+if [[ "${#SELECTED_SCENARIOS[@]}" -eq 0 ]]; then
+    mapfile -t SELECTED_SCENARIOS < <(jq -r '.smoke_scenarios[].scenario_id' "$CONTRACT_ARTIFACT")
+fi
+
+mkdir -p "$RUN_DIR"; OVERALL_RC=0
+for sid in "${SELECTED_SCENARIOS[@]}"; do run_scenario "$sid" || OVERALL_RC=1; done
+
+RUN_REPORT="${RUN_DIR}/run_report.json"
+cat >"$RUN_REPORT" <<JSON
+{
+  "schema_version": "$(json_escape "$(report_schema_version)")",
+  "contract_version": "$(json_escape "$(contract_version)")",
+  "run_dir": "$(json_escape "$RUN_DIR")",
+  "dry_run": $( [[ "$DRY_RUN" -eq 1 ]] && printf 'true' || printf 'false' ),
+  "results": [${RESULTS_JSON}],
+  "status": "$([ "$OVERALL_RC" -eq 0 ] && printf "passed" || printf "failed")"
+}
+JSON
+
+echo ""
+echo "==================================================================="
+echo "       CROSS-TRACK COMPOSITION SMOKE SUMMARY                       "
+echo "==================================================================="
+echo "  Run dir:   ${RUN_DIR}"
+echo "  Mode:      $([ "$DRY_RUN" -eq 1 ] && printf "DRY-RUN" || printf "EXECUTE")"
+echo "  Status:    $([ "$OVERALL_RC" -eq 0 ] && printf "PASSED" || printf "FAILED")"
+echo "==================================================================="
+
+exit "$OVERALL_RC"
